steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The main clause ends with "...collective defense," followed by a comma. What comes after that comma must grammatically attach to the main clause. A comma cannot join two independent clauses (comma splice), so the second element must be a non-finite participial phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — has pledged: finite verb — creates an independent clause joined by only a comma. Comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — pledging: present participle — forms a participial phrase that correctly modifies the subject of the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — pledges: finite verb — creates an independent clause joined by only a comma. Comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — is pledging: finite verb — creates an independent clause joined by only a comma. Comma splice. ✗
That is the correct answer.
